description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese-style short sword or large dagger, featuring a straight, single-edged blade with a dark metallic finish. The handle is wrapped in a red material, likely synthetic or cord, and capped with a gold-colored pommel and guard, providing both grip and decorative contrast. The guard is rectangular and black with gold accents, separating the blade from the handle. Accompanying the blade is a matching black sheath with gold-colored end caps, designed to securely house the blade when not in use. The overall design is sleek and functional, with minimal ornamentation, emphasizing utility and traditional aesthetics. The object is compact and appears well-balanced, suitable for carrying or display in an urban environment.
description: A Japanese-style short sword with a straight, single-edged blade, red-wrapped handle, gold accents, and a matching black sheath.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blade's tip, the red-wrapped handle, and the open end of the sheath.
description_view_1: Side profile displaying the full length of the blade and sheath, highlighting the straight blade and gold accents.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the pommel of the handle and the closed end of the sheath.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the blade and sheath alignment, with clear visibility of the handle's wrapping and guard.
attribute_handle_design: straight handle, red cord-wrapped, gold pommel, ergonomic grip
